description: Create a content slide on the BPOC Fund VI and Medicus Healthcare Solutions case study with a two-column layout, including bullet-list sections for opportunity overview, investment thesis, and investment update, plus a KPI grid of key investment metrics.

header.headline: Case Study: BPOC Fund VI and Medicus Healthcare Solutions
header.sub: Stapled co-investment / primary centered around fast-growing healthcare services platform

items.text: Established in 1996 and based in Chicago, IL
Focus on middle market businesses in the healthcare industry
PEG has invested with BPOC since 2018 and previously completed two secondary transactions that are performing well to date

items.sub: Leading national provider of locum tenens (physician) staffing services

items.text: Specialties include Hospitalist, Radiology, Anesthesiology, Emergency Medicine, Psychiatry and Surgery
Seasoned Fund VI portfolio exhibiting strong momentum at the time of investment
Attractive $1.5:$1 co-investment to primary commitment ratio
Strong financial profile with organic revenue growth, margin expansion and free cash flow conversion
Significant growth potential fueled by physician shortages and Medicus’ differentiated scale and unique offerings
Revenue and EBITDA are 12.8% and 23.0% ahead of budget, respectively
Outperformance driven by management’s core state strategy and a redefined production model, resulting in days worked rising
Strengthened forward visibility with a growing base of booked days
Performance driven primarily by Medicus and a follow-on in Atlas Clinical Research

footer.source: This example is included solely to illustrate strategies which have been utilized by PEG. It is expected that the portfolio will include a larger number of investments than the example set forth. There can be no guarantee or assurance that the portfolio will be able to make similar investments on similar terms in the future. Not all investments have had or will have similar results. The logos presented are registered trademarks of their respective companies. The inclusion of such companies should not be interpreted as a recommendation to buy or sell. 1 As of transaction close on 4/27/2024. 2 Investment updates as of 12/31/2025.
